text: The surgeon begins the laparoscopic hysterectomy by dividing the corneal support structures of the uterus using electrocautery. The peritoneal covering, known as the broad ligament, is then divided. The urinary bladder is carefully separated from the uterus to prevent damage. The uterine artery on the left side is divided, followed by the same procedure on the right side.

After dividing the uterine artery, the surgeon incises the vagina, separating the cervix from the vaginal tissue. The fallopian tubes are removed, while the ovaries may be preserved depending on the patient's age. The vagina is then sutured using absorbable sutures for reconstruction.
